mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding economic output and improving corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This relationship persists as long as the trend in IP is upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive 20-day returns during months of rising IP is not significantly above 50% (one-sided binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a threshold of 4.5% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to predict negative returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in both housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust domestic demand, which lifts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. The joint rise amplifies the signal beyond individual effects.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 6-month moving average, the subsequent 60-day forward return does not have a positive median (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a regression is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime where the CPI (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 300 and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 4%, the central bank's tightening bias compresses valuations and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for this joint condition is not significantly negative (one-sided p > 0.10), or the mean 20-day return in triggered periods is not lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) directly boosts aggregate income and consumption, providing a near-term tailwind for corporate profits and causing positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not positively correlated with the latest monthly PAYEMS change (Spearman rank correlation p > 0.05 over full sample), or the hit rate of positive returns when PAYEMS increases is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in retail sales (RSAFS) below 700,000 signals a sudden consumption contraction that quickly feeds into lower corporate earnings and negative investor sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When RSAFS falls below 700,000, the 20-day forward return is not negative in at least 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect simultaneous supply and demand weakening, which severely depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. The joint negative signal is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS are below their respective 3-month moving averages, the subsequent 20-day forward return does not have a negative median (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant in a linear model (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) to below 4% signals an easing monetary policy stance that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS is below 4%, the 5-day forward return is not positive in at least 55% of cases, or the average excess return over the unconditional mean is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
